note: Huge park with many paved, mostly level paths and several wheelchair-accessible subway stops around the perimeter (59 St-Columbus Circle, 72 St, 96 St). Bethesda Fountain, the Lake, playgrounds. Great for families.
Fully ADA-compliant; ramps throughout, accessible 86th-floor open-air deck with lowered viewing walls and binoculars, accessible restrooms. Strollers welcome (fold on the deck).

Entirely wheelchair accessible; memorial parapets have chamfered corners so seated visitors get the same view, free loaner chairs, free care-partner ticket, lifts to the underground museum. Moving, well-designed.

The ferry and both islands are wheelchair accessible (boarding ramps); Liberty Island grounds and the pedestal are reachable, but the crown is NOT (narrow spiral stairs). Ellis Island Immigration Museum fully accessible.
The entire experience is fully wheelchair accessible - Sky Pod elevators to the 100th-floor observatory, panoramic views. A great high-up win with no access compromises.
Flat, paved and fully rollable with pedestrian plazas, but intensely crowded day and night, which is tiring/overwhelming for seniors and slow going. Iconic to see.
Elevated park on a former rail line; step-free access via elevators at several entrances, flat and smooth throughout. A relaxed, accessible highlight - confirm which entrances have working lifts.
Striking transit hall and mall at the World Trade Center; fully accessible with lifts and vast level floors. Connects to accessible Fulton St subway.
World-class museum; step-free entry, elevators throughout, loaner wheelchairs, accessible restrooms. Vast - pace yourself. Pay-what-you-wish for NY State residents.
The elevated pedestrian promenade is walkable/rollable and flat in the middle, but the Manhattan-side approach has some stairs (seek the ramp entrance) and it is extremely crowded. Great views.
Free ferry with accessible boarding and great free Statue of Liberty views; a cheap accessible way to get on the water.
